subparagraph.chapeau: without loss of or reduction in—

clause.content: leave to which an employee is otherwise entitled under law; or
credit for time or service; and

subparagraph.content: that is not authorized under any other provision of law;
means an Executive agency (as defined in section 105 of this title);
includes the Department of Veterans Affairs; and
does not include the Government Accountability Office; and
has the meaning given the term in section 2105; and
does not include an intermittent employee who does not have an established regular tour of duty during the administrative workweek.

paragraph.heading: In general.—
paragraph.content: During any calendar year, an agency may place an employee in administrative leave for a period of not more than a total of 10 work days.

paragraph.heading: Records.—
paragraph.content: An agency shall record administrative leave separately from leave authorized under any other provision of law.

paragraph.heading: OPM regulations.—
paragraph.chapeau: Not later than 270 calendar days after the date of enactment of this section, the Director of the Office of Personnel Management shall—

subparagraph.content: prescribe regulations to carry out this section; and

subparagraph.chapeau: prescribe regulations that provide guidance to agencies regarding—

clause.content: acceptable agency uses of administrative leave; and

paragraph.heading: Agency action.—
paragraph.content: Not later than 270 calendar days after the date on which the Director of the Office of Personnel Management prescribes regulations under paragraph (1), each agency shall revise and implement the internal policies of the agency to meet the requirements of this section.

subsection.heading: Relation to Other Laws.—
subsection.content: Notwithstanding subsection (a) of section 7421 of title 38, this section shall apply to an employee described in subsection (b) of that section.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: The date of enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (c)(1), is the date of enactment of Pub. L. 114–328, which was approved Dec. 23, 2016.

note.heading: Sense of Congress
GAO Report
